---
title: "NemoClaw"
description: "OpenClaw inside an OpenShell sandbox — Bedrock inference with zero AWS API surface."
---

<Note>
**Experimental pack.** NemoClaw runs OpenClaw inside the OpenShell sandbox, which blocks all AWS API calls except Bedrock `InvokeModel`. Use when you want OpenClaw features (memory, channels, gateway) but zero AWS blast radius.
</Note>

<Warning>
**NemoClaw requires `--profile personal_assistant`.** The installer refuses any other profile. NemoClaw's whole point is running without AWS API access; builder or account_assistant would defeat the sandbox.
</Warning>

## When to use

- Want OpenClaw's features with maximum isolation
- Need Bedrock inference but the agent should NOT touch AWS resources
- Prefer the sandbox/OpenShell model

## What the pack installs

- OpenShell sandbox (`/opt/openshell/`)
- `bedrockify` dep (OpenAI-compatible Bedrock proxy) on port 8090
- `openclaw` running inside the sandbox
- Named sandbox (default: `loki-assistant`)
- Optional Telegram bridge if token/chat-ids provided

## Install — non-interactive

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
  --pack nemoclaw \
  --profile personal_assistant
```

Any other profile is rejected at install time.

## Pack parameters

| Param | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `region` | `us-east-1` | AWS region for Bedrock |
| `model` | `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6` | Bedrock model via bedrockify |
| `bedrockify-port` | `8090` | Where bedrockify listens |
| `sandbox-name` | `loki-assistant` | OpenShell sandbox name |
| `telegram-token` | — | Optional Telegram bot token |
| `allowed-chat-ids` | — | Comma-separated Telegram chat IDs |

These are threaded through bootstrap.sh's generic `--*` passthrough; advanced mode or manual pack invocation is the current way to override them.

## Resource requirements

| | Personal assistant |
|---|---|
| Instance | `t4g.medium` |
| Root volume | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 80 GB |

## First-run

```bash
aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>

# NemoClaw runs inside the sandbox
openshell exec loki-assistant openclaw --version
openshell exec loki-assistant openclaw agent --agent main -m "hello"
```

## Security model

OpenShell blocks all AWS SDK calls except Bedrock. An agent running malicious code inside NemoClaw cannot:

- Enumerate EC2/IAM/S3
- Assume roles
- Make non-Bedrock API calls

It **can** make Bedrock inference calls (that's the whole point). If you need to cut Bedrock too, use `personal_assistant` + no agent install, or use a different profile.

## Tear-down

Delete the stack. Sandbox + data volume go with it.
